skills.name: hermes-environment-migration
skills.description: Use when a Hermes environment must be safely migrated between machines with staged exports, integrity manifests, secret separation, selective imports, verification, and rollback.

skills.name: hermes-gateway-doctor
skills.description: Use when Hermes messaging gateway failures must be diagnosed across process state, adapters, credential posture, logs, delivery evidence, polling conflicts, and service persistence without automatic repair.

skills.name: hermes-profile-audit
skills.description: Use when a Hermes profile must be audited for role clarity, authority boundaries, configuration fit, skills, memory posture, credential scope, handoffs, and recurring operational failures.

skills.name: hermes-skill-audit
skills.description: Use when installed Hermes skills must be audited for overlap, staleness, broken references, usage-integrity problems, and dead weight without changing the installation.

skills.name: hermes-stack-doctor
skills.description: Use when a top-level, read-only Hermes health audit is needed across installation, updates, gateways, cron, profiles, skills, repositories, credential posture, persistence, and cost signals.

skills.name: hermes-token-audit
skills.description: Use when Hermes token usage, cost attribution, runaway sessions, cron consumption, or billing discrepancies must be investigated using privacy-preserving, schema-aware evidence.

skills.name: hermes-update-doctor
skills.description: Use when stuck, contradictory, blocked, or incomplete Hermes updates must be diagnosed across Git state, remotes, running processes, caches, and installed versions before recovery.

skills.name: interview-me
skills.description: Use when an adaptive, consent-based interview is needed before producing work because goals, constraints, preferences, tradeoffs, or success criteria are genuinely unclear.

skills.name: oss-tool-trust-audit
skills.description: Use when an open-source developer tool, package, CLI, agent, or MCP server must be evaluated for legitimacy, supply-chain risk, telemetry, dangerous capabilities, claim accuracy, and adoption fit.

skills.name: pre-build-feature-audit
skills.description: Use when a proposed open-source feature must be checked across source, history, branches, issues, pull requests, roadmaps, and contributor guidance before implementation begins.

skills.name: repo-readiness-audit
skills.description: Use when a user asks whether an identified repository is ready for further development, release work, a new feature, handoff, or a new contributor, requiring a disciplined read-only audit before an evidence-backed verdict.

skills.name: x-analytics-import
skills.description: Use when X Analytics CSV exports must be inspected, validated, normalized, imported, or compared through a repeatable private-by-default workflow.

skills.name: x-post-writer
skills.description: Use when drafting, rewriting, or repurposing short-form X content, including single posts, quote posts, replies, threads, launches, and personal stories, with source fidelity and claim verification built in.
